task.objective: First slice of the post-bootstrap 'branch junctions, UVs and roots' milestone: base flare with arc-length decay (trunk/root flare and collar junctions), lobed radial cross-sections with analytic in-plane normals fading above the flare region, ground-clamped root spines, and per-node bark UV phase from named random streams.

design_authority: docs/project_canopy_design_pack/09_BRANCH_MESHING.md (Radius and flare; Ring generation; Junction strategies: collar; Bark UVs)
outputs: src/geometry: SpineSample.lobe_scale, SweepOptions lobes/uv_v_offset, lobed ring generation with analytic normals
src/evaluation: spine.flare.* properties, spine.ground.level clamp, mesh.uv.random_phase
tests: lobed-ring topology/extrema, uv offset, flare decay, ground clamp, per-node UV phase determinism
samples: oak roots + buttressed bases across all three showcase trees

validation.result: 100% tests passed (12 suites) on both presets

risks: Collar is flare-based (overlap+flare); welded ring stitch and implicit remesh junction strategies still pending
Ground clamp flattens the growth direction rather than solving contact; proper terrain collision arrives with geometry forces
Root meshes can dip radius-deep below the clamp plane (spine is clamped, surface is not)
